table_description: Stores medical diagnoses assigned during patient visits, including severity and diagnostic codes.

columns.name: diagnosis_id
columns.description: Unique system-generated identifier for each diagnosis record.

columns.name: visit_id
columns.description: Foreign key referencing the visit during which the diagnosis was made.

columns.name: diagnosis_code
columns.description: Standardized medical diagnosis code (e.g., ICD code) representing the condition.

columns.name: diagnosis_description
columns.description: Textual description of the diagnosed medical condition.

columns.name: severity_level
columns.description: Indicates the severity of the diagnosed condition (e.g., mild, moderate, severe).

table_description:
Stores healthcare domain business knowledge used by the Intent Agent to interpret user queries. 
The rules describe healthcare analytical concepts and insights in natural language. 
They intentionally avoid referencing database tables, columns, or SQL logic to prevent 
biasing downstream agents responsible for schema selection and SQL generation.

columns.name: rule_id
columns.description: Unique identifier assigned to each healthcare business rule.

columns.name: concept_name
columns.description: High-level healthcare concept or analytical theme represented by the rule, such as patient activity, treatment patterns, or healthcare service utilization.

columns.name: description
columns.description:
Neutral natural-language explanation of the healthcare concept. 
This field describes the concept without referencing database schema elements, implementation details, or SQL logic.

columns.name: insight
columns.description: Healthcare insight explaining why the concept is important for analysis, reporting, or operational understanding.

columns.name: keywords
columns.description:
List of keywords or phrases associated with the healthcare concept. 
These keywords help semantic retrieval systems match user queries with relevant business rules.

columns.name: created_at
columns.description:
Timestamp indicating when the healthcare business rule was created. 
Useful for governance, auditing, and lifecycle management.

table_description: Stores demographic and registration details of patients receiving healthcare services.

columns.name: patient_id
columns.description: Unique system-generated identifier assigned to each patient.

columns.name: first_name
columns.description: Patient's legal first name.

columns.name: last_name
columns.description: Patient's legal last name.

columns.name: date_of_birth
columns.description: Date of birth of the patient used for age-based analysis and eligibility checks.

columns.name: gender
columns.description: Self-reported gender of the patient.

columns.name: city
columns.description: City of residence of the patient.

columns.name: state
columns.description: State of residence of the patient.

columns.name: insurance_type
columns.description: Type of insurance coverage used by the patient (e.g., private, government, uninsured).

columns.name: registration_date
columns.description: Date when the patient was first registered in the healthcare system.

table_description: Records individual hospital or clinic visits made by patients, including department, visit type, and associated costs.

columns.name: visit_id
columns.description: Unique system-generated identifier for each patient visit.

columns.name: patient_id
columns.description: Foreign key referencing the patient who attended the visit.

columns.name: admission_date
columns.description: Date when the patient was admitted for the visit.

columns.name: discharge_date
columns.description: Date when the patient was discharged from the visit.

columns.name: department
columns.description: Medical department responsible for the visit (e.g., cardiology, emergency, oncology).

columns.name: visit_type
columns.description: Classification of visit such as inpatient, outpatient, or emergency.

columns.name: total_cost
columns.description: Total billed cost associated with the visit, including treatments and services.
